label: HR 1917 Great Lakes Mass Marking Act

comments.text: ✅ Passed the House My bill would implement mass marking programs for hatchery-produced fish across the entire Great Lakes region, strengthening hatchery operations and fishery management.

The House Committee on Natural Resources Subcommittee for Water, Wildlife, and Fisheries held a hearing to discuss the Great Lakes Mass Marking Program, which would increase tagging of hatchery-raised fish to distinguish them from wild fish. See the full story in our weekly update
BILL UPDATE: H.1917: Great Lakes Mass Marking Program Act of 2025 ACTION: Subcommittee Hearings Held #congress
H.R. 1917 by Rep. Debbie Dingell [D-MI6] passed the House yesterday (→Senate). Great Lakes Mass Marking Program Act of 2025 🏛️

Last week, the Great Lakes Mass Marking Act of 2025 was advanced out of Committee by unanimous consent. The bill would expand mass marking, an essential practice for fisheries across the Great Lakes. Read more in our Weekly Update here: conta.cc/4eqxulT

Michigan lawmakers Rep. Debbie Dingell and Rep. Tim Walberg introduced H.R. 6053, the Save Great Lakes Fish Act of 2025. This bipartisan bill would authorize $500 million over 10 years for the U.S section of the Great Lakes Fishery Commission. More here:

Last week The House passed the American Water Stewardship Act. Led by members of the Great Lakes Task Force, the AWSA contains reauthorizations for the GLRI Act, BEACH Act, and ESTUARIES Act, which would be reauthorized through FY2031. Read more: conta.cc/4sHC7OX

The Senate Commerce Committee hearing last week discussed lack of investment in coastal mapping. Last year, Reps. Lisa McClain (R-MI) and Debbie Dingell (D-MI) introduced The Great Lakes Mapping Act of 2025, a $250M investment to map the Great Lakes. More here: www.nemw.org/issue-spotli...

Yeas: 207 D, 153 R
Nays: 57 R
Not Voting: 8 R, 5 D #HR1917 #congress119 #house #democrat #congress
The House is beginning up to 40 mins debate on H.R. 1917 – Great Lakes Mass Marking Program Act of 2025, as amended (Sponsored by / Natural Resources Committee).

The House meets at 10:00 AM for Morning Hour and 12:00 PM for legislative business. It will consider two suspension bills (H.R. 3937 – Wabeno Economic Development Act, as amended and H.R. 1917 – Great Lakes Mass Marking Program Act of 2025). First/Last Votes: 4:30 PM

NEMWI has updated its Great Lakes Tracker with recent Congressional action. Two bipartisan bills were introduced in the Senate to reauthorize the USGS Great Lakes Science Center and establish the Great Lakes Mass Marking program. Read more here:

Senator Gary Peters from Michigan recently cosponsored the Great Lakes Fisheries Research Reauthorization Act and the Great Lakes Mass Marking Program Act. Both of these pieces of legislation would increase protection for the Great Lakes and authorize funding for projects. 1/4
